Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, low CO₂, and significant proteinuria, indicating severe renal dysfunction, possibly acute or chronic kidney disease. Urine findings (glucose, ketones, protein, low specific gravity, brown color, ammoniacal odor) suggest impaired renal concentrating ability and possible metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c with normoglycemia may reflect recent changes in glucose metabolism. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended.
